Germany 1-1 Paraguay
Brazil 2-1 Japan
South Africa 0-1 Canada

stories.headline: How India's cricket team can qualify for the LA 2028 Olympics — explained
stories.what: With cricket set to make its Olympic return at the Los Angeles 2028 Games, a detailed explainer lays out exactly what the Indian men's cricket team needs to do to earn qualification. The qualification pathway involves a combination of ICC rankings, regional qualifiers, and tournament performance, making it a complex but achievable goal for a side of India's calibre. The LA 2028 Olympics will be the sport's first appearance at the Games since 1900.
stories.lens: An Olympic gold medal in cricket would be the biggest prize the sport has ever offered — understanding India's qualification route matters for every fan who wants to see Rohit Sharma or Virat Kohli compete at the Games.
stories.article:
Cricket's return to the Olympic stage at Los Angeles 2028 has opened up a new frontier for the sport, and the Indian men's team's path to qualification is now being mapped out in detail. The Games will mark cricket's first Olympic appearance in over a century, making the qualification race one of the most consequential processes in the sport's modern history.

The qualification pathway, as explained by olympics.com, involves ICC rankings and structured competitive routes that will determine which nations earn the coveted spots in the LA 2028 tournament. India, given their standing as the world's most powerful cricketing nation by both playing strength and commercial weight, are expected to be frontrunners — but the process is not automatic and will require consistent performances in the right tournaments.

The inclusion of cricket at the Olympics is a transformative moment for the sport globally, opening doors to audiences and athletes in nations where cricket has traditionally had limited reach. For Indian players, an Olympic medal would add a dimension to their legacy that no ICC title — however prestigious — can replicate.

For the BCCI and Indian fans, the LA 2028 timeline also injects fresh urgency into bilateral and ICC competitions between now and the Games, as each result could have a bearing on India's ultimate Olympic qualification. The explainer makes clear that the journey starts now.
key_facts: Cricket returning to Olympics at LA 2028 for first time since 1900
India's qualification depends on ICC rankings and regional routes
Olympics.com published a detailed explainer on the qualification pathway

stories.headline: Madan Lal warns India: 'Beating England in T20Is won't be easy'
stories.what: Former India cricketer and 1983 World Cup hero Madan Lal has cautioned the Indian team not to underestimate England ahead of their upcoming T20I series, saying it 'won't be easy'. His warning reflects the respect commanded by England's white-ball setup, which has been one of the most dangerous T20 outfits in world cricket in recent years. The message comes as India prepare for a high-profile bilateral series with fresh selection and form questions in the air.
stories.lens: When a World Cup winner warns that India faces a stern test at home, it's a timely reminder that complacency could be costly — and that this England series is a genuine examination of the team's post-World Cup reset.
stories.article:
Madan Lal, the 1983 World Cup winner whose name is synonymous with India's greatest cricket triumph, has issued a word of caution to the current generation ahead of the India vs England T20I series 2026. Speaking to the Times of India, the former pacer said simply: 'It won't be easy.'

Lal's warning carries the weight of experience. England's white-ball cricket has undergone a transformation in recent years and they arrive as a team that knows how to win in subcontinental conditions — as their previous tours have shown. India, despite the home advantage, cannot afford to approach this series with anything less than full preparation and application.

The caution from Lal comes at a sensitive time for Indian cricket. Both the men's and women's sides have faced criticism for underperformance in ICC events, and the England series will be one of the first major tests of how the team and selectors have responded to those disappointments. A strong showing against quality opposition would do much to restore confidence.

For India's batters and bowlers alike, the series is an opportunity to make statements — and Madan Lal's message is a timely prompt that statements are earned against sides as capable as England, not assumed.
key_facts: Madan Lal warns India the England T20I series 'won't be easy'
England are a formidable white-ball side with subcontinental experience
Series comes amid scrutiny of India's performances in ICC events

stories.headline: India vs England T20I series 2026: Full schedule, squads, venues and how to watch
stories.what: The upcoming India vs England T20I series 2026 has been mapped out, with the full schedule, complete squads from both sides, match venues, IST timings, and live streaming details now available. The series is set to be a marquee contest between two of world cricket's strongest T20 nations. With India preparing to reassert themselves in bilateral cricket after a mixed run in ICC tournaments, the England series takes on added importance.
stories.lens: For cricket fans across India, the England T20I series is the next big date on the calendar — knowing the venues, timings, and how to stream the matches helps millions plan their viewing well in advance.
stories.article:
The full blueprint for the India vs England T20I series 2026 has been released, covering the schedule, venues, IST timings, squad lists from both nations, and live streaming options for Indian viewers. The series represents one of the most anticipated bilateral cricket engagements of the year, pitting two powerhouses of the shortest format against each other.

India will be eager to put behind a difficult run in recent ICC events, and a home series against England — one of the most technically equipped T20 sides in the world — will serve as both a test of character and a platform for new players to stake their claims. England, themselves a formidable white-ball unit, will arrive as serious challengers with no intention of making it comfortable for the hosts.

Former India fast bowler Madan Lal has already sounded a note of caution ahead of the series, warning that facing England will not be easy and urging the team not to take the opposition lightly. His message reflects a broader anxiety that India must not let their home advantage lull them into complacency.

With venues, timings, and streaming details now confirmed, fans across the country can plan their schedules around the series, which promises to be a high-octane contest and a crucial staging post for both squads as they build towards future ICC events.
key_facts: India vs England T20I series 2026 schedule and squads announced
Venues, IST timings and live streaming details confirmed
Madan Lal warns India the series 'won't be easy'

stories.headline: The hard questions Indian women's cricket must answer after another T20 World Cup failure
stories.what: Moneycontrol examines the deeper structural and selection challenges facing Indian women's cricket after the team's latest disappointing T20 World Cup campaign. The piece interrogates whether the same systemic issues — inconsistent selection, over-reliance on certain players, and gaps in bench strength — continue to hold the side back from competing with the world's best. With the Asian Games and bilateral series ahead, the road to redemption begins now.
stories.lens: Indian women's cricket has immense talent but keeps falling short at the biggest moments — understanding why, and what changes are needed, directly affects whether fans will see a genuine title challenge at the next World Cup.
stories.article:
Indian women's cricket faces yet another reckoning after yet another early exit from the T20 World Cup, and the questions being asked now are sharper and more urgent than ever before. Despite having a generation of talented players, the team has consistently failed to translate promise into ICC tournament victories, and patience among fans and analysts is wearing thin.

Moneycontrol's analysis points to a range of structural concerns — inconsistency in selection policy, a lack of robust bench depth, and the mental and tactical gaps that separate India from the top T20 sides. The team has often relied heavily on a handful of performers, and when those players underperform, the entire campaign unravels with startling speed.

The timing of this reflection is critical. With the Asian Games 2026 squad already announced and the India vs England bilateral calendar looming, the women's team and its management have a series of competitive opportunities to course-correct before the next major ICC event. Each match in this period will be scrutinised for evidence of genuine progress.

For Indian women's cricket to truly break through, analysts argue that a more settled selection approach, greater investment in domestic structures, and a willingness to back young players through difficult patches will all be essential. Another T20 World Cup disappointment of the same kind, many believe, would simply be unacceptable.
key_facts: India women exited the T20 World Cup with a disappointing campaign
Structural issues: selection inconsistency and bench depth flagged
Asian Games 2026 and England series are next opportunities to rebuild

stories.headline: ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026: Where the points table stands
stories.what: The ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 points table is being tracked by Cricbuzz as the tournament progresses, reflecting the current standings of all competing nations. The table offers a real-time snapshot of which teams are advancing, which are on the bubble, and which have been knocked out of contention. India's position in the standings will be of particular interest to millions of fans.
stories.lens: The T20 World Cup points table determines India's fate in the tournament — knowing where the team stands directly shapes whether fans have a knockout stage to look forward to.
stories.article:
The ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 is underway, and the points table — as tracked by Cricbuzz — provides a live picture of where every competing nation stands in the race to the knockout rounds. With multiple groups running simultaneously, the standings shift rapidly and carry enormous consequences for each team's campaign.

India's position in the table is the most closely watched by the cricketing public, but every result across the groups feeds into the broader narrative of who is emerging as a genuine title contender and who faces an early flight home. The top teams from each group advance, making every group-stage match high-stakes.

The T20 World Cup 2026 has already seen upsets and dominant performances, and the points table captures those fluctuations in real time. Teams with superior net run rates can find themselves ahead of sides with equal points, adding an extra tactical layer to how teams approach their remaining fixtures.

For Indian fans, the table is both a scoreboard and a roadmap — it tells them exactly what results are needed in upcoming matches for India to progress. With the knockout stage in sight, each update to the standings brings either relief or anxiety to hundreds of millions following the tournament.
key_facts: ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 points table updated on Cricbuzz
Group-stage standings determine knockout qualification
Net run rate can separate teams level on points

stories.headline: South Indian cinema's controversies in first half of 2026: Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' censor row and 'Drishyam 3' among flashpoints
stories.what: The first half of 2026 has seen South Indian cinema hit by a series of notable controversies, with a censor-related row surrounding Vijay's Jana Nayagan and developments around Drishyam 3 among the most prominent. The Times of India has compiled the controversies that marked the period, reflecting the heightened public and regulatory scrutiny that the industry now faces. The incidents span certification disputes, creative conflicts, and broader cultural flashpoints in Telugu, Tamil, and Malayalam film industries.
stories.lens: South Indian cinema's growing national and global footprint means its controversies now command mainstream attention across India — what happens in Chennai or Hyderabad's film corridors shapes the entertainment conversation for hundreds of millions of viewers.
stories.article:
The first six months of 2026 have been unusually turbulent for South Indian cinema, with a string of controversies touching some of the industry's biggest names and most anticipated projects. A round-up by The Times of India captures the breadth of the friction, from censor-related battles to disputes around sequels and creative decisions.

Among the most high-profile incidents was the censor row surrounding Jana Nayagan, the film starring Vijay — the Tamil superstar who has also made a high-profile entry into politics. Any dispute involving Vijay's films now carries an additional layer of political scrutiny, making the certification row particularly charged. Drishyam 3, the next installment in the massively popular Malayalam thriller franchise, also figured in the list of controversies, though the specific nature of its dispute was part of the broader compilation.

The range of incidents reflects the complex environment in which South Indian filmmakers now operate — navigating certification boards, cultural sensitivities, star power, and a polarised public discourse that can quickly amplify any friction into a national story.

With South Indian films routinely crossing Rs 100-500 crore at the box office and drawing pan-India audiences, the stakes attached to these controversies are higher than ever. Industry watchers will be hoping the second half of 2026 is less turbulent.
key_facts: Jana Nayagan starring Vijay embroiled in censor row
Drishyam 3 among South Indian controversies of early 2026
Controversies span Tamil, Malayalam and other South Indian industries

stories.headline: Ikka trailer: Sunny Deol returns as a fierce lawyer in courtroom face-off with Akshaye Khanna
stories.what: The trailer for Ikka has been released, showing Sunny Deol back in a courtroom setting as a fierce lawyer who strikes an unexpected deal with Akshaye Khanna's character. The film marks Deol's return to the legal drama genre, territory familiar to fans of his iconic 1993 hit Damini. Deol has separately revealed that he had long tried to make a Damini sequel before eventually channelling that energy into Ikka instead.
stories.lens: Sunny Deol's return to courtroom drama — the genre that gave him one of his biggest hits — is a direct nostalgia play for audiences who grew up on Damini, making Ikka one of the more keenly watched Bollywood releases for that generation.
stories.article:
The trailer for Ikka is out, and it sees Sunny Deol back where many of his fans most want him — in a courtroom, playing a fierce, uncompromising lawyer. The clip sets up a high-stakes legal battle between Deol's character and Akshaye Khanna, with the two apparently entering a shocking deal that forms the dramatic spine of the film.

The release of the trailer comes alongside Deol's own candid admission that Ikka is, in many ways, a spiritual successor to ambitions that began with Damini. The actor revealed that he had tried hard to develop a direct sequel to the 1993 Rajkumar Santoshi classic, which won him a Filmfare Award and remains one of his most celebrated performances, but those efforts ultimately did not materialise.

Ikka thus represents the closest Deol fans will get to that courtroom universe — a return to the archetype without being a sequel. Akshaye Khanna, who has enjoyed a strong run of acclaimed performances in recent years, is set up as a formidable opposite number, lending the trailer a sharp adversarial energy.

The film is generating early buzz on the strength of the lead pairing and the nostalgia factor. Whether it can translate trailer enthusiasm into box-office numbers will be the next test for Deol's ongoing Bollywood comeback.
key_facts: Ikka trailer released featuring Sunny Deol as a lawyer
Deol faces off against Akshaye Khanna in the film
Deol had tried to make Damini 2 before Ikka came together

stories.headline: Salman Khan set to leave Galaxy Apartments after 52 years, gets approval for new 6-storey home
stories.what: Salman Khan, who has lived at Galaxy Apartments in Mumbai's Bandra for 52 years, is reportedly set to move out after receiving approval for a new 6-storey residence. The iconic address has long been synonymous with the actor and draws thousands of fans each year, particularly on his birthday. The approved multi-storey home will mark the end of one of Bollywood's most famous addresses and the beginning of a new chapter for the superstar.
stories.lens: Galaxy Apartments is one of Bollywood's most iconic addresses — Salman Khan's departure after more than five decades closes a chapter that is part of Mumbai's cultural fabric, and the new home will redefine how fans connect with their biggest star.
stories.article:
Salman Khan is preparing to leave Galaxy Apartments, the Bandra address he has called home for 52 years and one of the most recognisable residential landmarks in all of Bollywood. According to a report in The Indian Express, the actor has received approval for a new 6-storey home, setting the stage for his eventual departure from the building that has defined his public persona for decades.

Galaxy Apartments has become far more than a residence. It is a pilgrimage site of sorts for Bollywood fans — the spot where thousands gather on Eid and on Khan's birthday hoping for a glimpse of the star on his balcony. The address has featured in news coverage, fan videos, and cultural conversations about Mumbai and Hindi cinema for as long as most fans can remember.

The shift to a purpose-built 6-storey home suggests Khan is investing in a more private, secure, and custom-designed living arrangement — not unusual for a star of his stature who has faced significant security concerns in recent years. The approval of the new building marks the beginning of what is likely to be a phased move.

For Mumbaikars and Bollywood watchers, the departure will be the end of an era. Whatever the new address becomes, it will take decades — if ever — to acquire the cultural weight of Galaxy.
key_facts: Salman Khan has lived at Galaxy Apartments for 52 years
New 6-storey home has received building approval
Galaxy Apartments in Bandra is one of Bollywood's most iconic addresses

stories.headline: Shraddha Kapoor's 'Eetha' faces title dispute as Vithabai Narayangaonkar's family demands change
stories.what: Shraddha Kapoor's upcoming film Eetha has run into trouble ahead of its release, with the family of renowned Lavani folk artist Vithabai Narayangaonkar demanding that the makers change the film's title. The family's objection suggests a perceived association between the film's title and the legacy of the celebrated Maharashtrian folk singer, whose name and memory are considered culturally significant. The dispute adds to the pre-release complications for the film.
stories.lens: Title disputes can delay or derail a film's release and marketing — for Shraddha Kapoor, whose last few films needed strong box-office performances, any legal or cultural friction ahead of Eetha's launch is an unwelcome development.
stories.article:
Shraddha Kapoor's forthcoming film Eetha has found itself in a controversy even before its release, with the family of Vithabai Narayangaonkar — the iconic Maharashtrian Lavani and folk artist — formally demanding that the production change the film's title.

The family's objection centres on a perceived connection between the title and the legacy of Vithabai Narayangaonkar, who is a deeply revered figure in Maharashtra's cultural landscape. Her contributions to Lavani and Tamasha folk traditions have earned her an enduring place in the state's artistic heritage, and her family appears concerned that the title could create an unintended or misleading association.

The filmmakers have not yet publicly responded to the demand, leaving open the question of whether the title will be retained, modified, or replaced entirely. Title changes at a late stage in a film's production can have ripple effects on marketing, promotional material, and trade bookings.

For Shraddha Kapoor, who has been working to consolidate her box-office standing with a string of commercially focused projects, this is an unwanted distraction. How the producers navigate the dispute — through dialogue with the family or potentially through legal channels — will determine whether the film's release timeline is affected.
key_facts: Vithabai Narayangaonkar's family demands title change for Eetha
Narayangaonkar was a celebrated Maharashtrian Lavani folk artist
Film stars Shraddha Kapoor

stories.headline: Diljit Dosanjh's 'Main Vaapas Aaunga' crosses Rs 70 crore worldwide on Day 17, surpasses 'Jab We Met'
stories.what: Imtiaz Ali and Diljit Dosanjh's film Main Vaapas Aaunga has crossed Rs 70 crore in worldwide box office collections by its 17th day of release, surpassing the lifetime earnings of the beloved 2007 romantic classic Jab We Met. The milestone marks a significant commercial achievement for the film, which continues to draw audiences nearly three weeks after its opening. The performance underlines the sustained pull of both Dosanjh's stardom and Imtiaz Ali's brand of storytelling.
stories.lens: Crossing Rs 70 crore worldwide while beating a Bollywood benchmark like Jab We Met signals that Diljit Dosanjh is now a reliable box-office draw in mainstream Hindi cinema — a shift that matters for how Punjabi stars are cast and backed in big-budget films.
stories.article:
Main Vaapas Aaunga has crossed Rs 70 crore in worldwide box office collections on its 17th day of release, surpassing the lifetime earnings of Imtiaz Ali's own 2007 cult hit Jab We Met — a benchmark that carries emotional weight for fans of the filmmaker.

The film, directed by Imtiaz Ali and starring Diljit Dosanjh, has shown strong legs at the box office, defying the typical mid-week dip that afflicts most Bollywood releases. Crossing a milestone associated with one of Hindi cinema's most beloved films adds a layer of symbolic significance to what is already a solid commercial run.

For Diljit Dosanjh, the numbers further cement his standing as a frontline Bollywood star, following his runaway success on the concert circuit and a string of well-received film appearances. The collaboration with Imtiaz Ali — who had previously worked with stars like Ranbir Kapoor and Shah Rukh Khan — was itself seen as a vote of confidence in Dosanjh's mainstream appeal.

The film's trajectory will now be watched closely to see whether it can sustain collections through the coming weekend and push further toward the coveted Rs 100 crore mark globally.
key_facts: Main Vaapas Aaunga crosses Rs 70 crore worldwide by Day 17
Film surpasses lifetime collection of Jab We Met
Directed by Imtiaz Ali, starring Diljit Dosanjh
